summary:
The pes anserine complex involves tendons from multiple muscles attaching at the knee; other body parts lacking such specific muscular arrangements make this unique feature hard to replicate exactly elsewise within human musculoskeletal system contextually presented choices leave TMJ as least suitable among listed ones by process elimination method applied herein. \boxed{TMJ}

The summary has been condensed into two coherent statements focusing only on critical aspects pertinent directly addressing core query requirements regarding comparative anatomical features between mentioned regions concerning their respective muscle-tendon complexes' presence or absence therein accurately reflecting initial analysis conducted above while adhering strictly per instructions stipulated earlier. Agent2 concluded based primarily upon exclusionary logic ruling out all but TMJ which was deemed less appropriate than others even though exact parallel wasn't found anywhere making final selection somewhat arbitrary under strict interpretation constraints imposed initially during problem solving phase outlined previously. 

Key Points Summarized:

1. Pes Anserine Complex includes tendinous inserts from several thigh muscles converging near distal femur/tibial region forming distinct functional unit.
